data.current_summary: Disassociate an authenticated domain from a given user.
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to disassociate a specific authenticated domain from a subuser.**

Authenticated domains can be associated with (i.e. assigned to) subusers from a parent account. This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's domain. To associate an authenticated domain with a subuser, the parent account must first authenticate and validate the domain. The parent may then associate the authenticated domain via the subuser management tools.


Note that if you used the [`/v3/whitelabel/domains/{domain_id}/subuser:add` endpoint](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/domain-authentication/associate-an-authenticated-domain-with-a-subuser-multiple) to add multiple domains to the subuser, you should use the [`/v3/whitelabel/domains/{domain_id}/subuser` endpoint](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/domain-authentication/disassociate-an-authenticated-domain-from-a-subuser-multiple) to disassociate those domains.
data.description_suggestion: Removes the association between an authenticated domain and a subuser, preventing the subuser from sending email using that domain. Requires the domain ID and subuser ID as query parameters. The parent account must have previously associated the domain with the subuser. After disassociation, the subuser will no longer have access to send mail from that domain and will fall back to other available domains in the domain hierarchy. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful disassociation. Note: For domains added via the bulk add endpoint, use the bulk disassociate endpoint instead.
data.reasoning: The current description provides context about domain association but lacks critical operational details. Missing: (1) specific input parameters required (domain_id, subuser_id), (2) expected response/status code, (3) side effects of disassociation (subuser loses access, falls back to hierarchy). Added these details to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation, and what to expect after execution.

data.current_summary: List all the authenticated domains associated with the given user.
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve all of the authenticated domains that have been assigned to a specific subuser.**


This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's domain. Authenticated domains can be associated with (i.e. assigned to) subusers from a parent account, and a subuser can have up to five associated domains. 

To associate an authenticated domain with a subuser, the parent account must first authenticate and validate the domain. The parent may then associate the authenticated domain via the subuser management tools.


When selecting a domain to send email from, SendGrid checks for domains in the following order and chooses the first one that appears in the hierarchy: 
1. Domain assigned by the subuser that matches the email's `From` address domain. 
2. The subuser's default domain. 
3. Domain assigned by the parent user that matches the `From` address domain. 
4. Parent user's default domain. 
5. sendgrid.net
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all authenticated domains currently associated with a specific subuser. Requires the subuser ID as a query parameter. Returns an array of authenticated domain objects, each containing domain details such as domain name, verification status, and default domain flag. A subuser can have up to five associated domains. The returned domains are used by SendGrid in a specific hierarchy when selecting which domain to send email from: (1) subuser domain matching the From address, (2) subuser's default domain, (3) parent domain matching the From address, (4) parent's default domain, (5) sendgrid.net. Useful for verifying domain assignments and determining available sending domains for a subuser.
data.reasoning: The current description provides good context about domain hierarchy but lacks specific operational details. Missing: (1) required input parameter (subuser_id), (2) response structure (array of domain objects with specific fields), (3) practical use cases for AI agents. Added these details to clarify what parameters are needed, what data structure is returned, and when an AI agent should use this operation.

data.current_summary: Disassociate an authenticated domain from a given user for users with up to five associated domains.
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to disassociate a specific authenticated domain from a subuser, for users with up to five associated domains.**


This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's domain. Authenticated domains can be associated with (i.e. assigned to) subusers kknt, and a subuser can have up to five associated authenticated domains. 

You can dissociate an authenticated domain from any subuser that has one or more authenticated domains using this endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Remove authenticated domain association from subuser
data.description_suggestion: Removes an authenticated domain association from a specified subuser. Requires the domain ID and subuser ID as path parameters. The subuser must have at least one associated authenticated domain. After disassociation, the subuser will no longer be able to send mail using that domain and will fall back to the parent account's domain hierarchy. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful removal.

data.current_summary: Associate an authenticated domain with a given user, for up to five domains.
data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to associate a specific authenticated domain with a subuser. It can be used to associate up to five authenticated domains.**


This functionality allows subusers to send mail using their parent's domain. Authenticated domains can be associated with (i.e. assigned to) subusers from a parent account. To associate an authenticated domain with a subuser, the parent account must first authenticate and validate the domain. The parent may then associate the authenticated domain via the subuser management tools.


A subuser can have up to five associated authenticated domains. To see the domains that have already been associated with this user, you can [use the API to list the domains currently associated with the subuser](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/domain-authentication/list-the-authenticated-domain-associated-with-a-subuser-multiple).


When selecting a domain to send email from, SendGrid checks for domains in the following order and chooses the first one that appears in the hierarchy: 
1. Domain assigned by the subuser that matches the email's `From` address domain. 
2. The subuser's default domain. 
3. Domain assigned by the parent user that matches the `From` address domain. 
4. Parent user's default domain. 
5. sendgrid.net
data.description_suggestion: Assigns an authenticated domain to a subuser, enabling the subuser to send mail using the parent account's domain. Requires the domain ID in the path and subuser ID in the request body. The domain must be authenticated and validated by the parent account first. A subuser can have up to five associated domains. Returns a 201 Created response with the association details. Domain selection follows a priority hierarchy: subuser domain matching From address, subuser default, parent domain matching From address, parent default, then sendgrid.net.
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about the action and doesn't clarify it's assigning to a subuser. Description is verbose and lacks clarity on required inputs and response format. Improved version uses action-oriented language, specifies required parameters, clarifies prerequisites, indicates response code, and concisely explains the domain selection hierarchy for AI agent clarity.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve the default authentication for a domain.**

When creating or updating a domain authentication, you can set the domain as a default. The default domain will be used to send all mail. If you have multiple authenticated domains, the authenticated domain matching the domain of the From address will be used, and the default will be overridden.

This endpoint will return a default domain and its details only if a default is set. You are not required to set a default. If you do not set a default domain, this endpoint will return general information about your domain authentication status.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the default authenticated domain for the authenticated user or subuser. Returns the default domain and its authentication details if one has been set. If no default domain is configured, returns general domain authentication status information. The default domain is used as the fallback when sending mail if no domain matches the From address. Returns a 200 OK response with the default domain object or authentication status.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get the default authentication' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate it's retrieving a domain. Description is verbose and confusing about what is returned in different scenarios. Improved summary clarifies the resource being retrieved. Enhanced description clearly explains the two possible return scenarios, the purpose of the default domain, and specifies the response code for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Email DNS records to a co-worker
data.current_description:
**This endpoint is used to share DNS records with a colleagues**

Use this endpoint to send SendGrid-generated DNS record information to a co-worker so they can enter it into your DNS provider to validate your domain and link branding. 

What type of records are sent will depend on whether you have chosen Automated Security or not. When using Automated Security, SendGrid provides you with three CNAME records. If you turn Automated Security off, you are instead given TXT and MX records.

If you pass a `link_id` to this endpoint, the generated email will supply the DNS records necessary to complete [Link Branding](https://sendgrid.com/docs/ui/account-and-settings/how-to-set-up-link-branding/) setup. If you pass a `domain_id` to this endpoint, the generated email will supply the DNS records needed to complete [Domain Authentication](https://sendgrid.com/docs/ui/account-and-settings/how-to-set-up-domain-authentication/). Passing both IDs will generate an email with the records needed to complete both setup steps.

You can retrieve all your domain IDs from the returned `id` fields for each domain using the "List all authenticated domains" endpoint. You can retrieve all of your link IDs using the "Retrieve all branded links" endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Send DNS records to colleague for domain validation
data.description_suggestion: Sends SendGrid-generated DNS records to a specified email address to facilitate domain and link branding setup. Accepts either a `domain_id` for Domain Authentication or `link_id` for Link Branding setup, or both. The DNS records sent depend on your Automated Security setting: Automated Security provides CNAME records, while manual mode provides TXT and MX records. Requires the recipient's email address as input. Returns confirmation of email delivery.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Email DNS records to a co-worker' is vague and doesn't clarify the purpose. Description has grammatical error ('colleagues' should be 'colleague') and lacks clarity on required inputs and outputs. Improved summary specifies the action and purpose. Enhanced description clarifies what records are sent based on security settings, required parameters (domain_id or link_id), and expected output, making it more actionable for AI agents.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to authenticate a domain.**

If you are authenticating a domain for a subuser, you have two options:
1. Use the "username" parameter. This allows you to authenticate a domain on behalf of your subuser. This means the subuser is able to see and modify the authenticated domain.
2. Use the Association workflow (see Associate Domain section). This allows you to authenticate a domain created by the parent to a subuser. This means the subuser will default to the assigned domain, but will not be able to see or modify that authenticated domain. However, if the subuser authenticates their own domain it will overwrite the assigned domain.
data.description_suggestion: Authenticates a domain for your account or on behalf of a subuser. Requires domain information in the request body. For subuser authentication, you can either use the "username" parameter to authenticate a domain on behalf of a subuser (allowing them to see and modify it), or use the Association workflow to assign a parent-authenticated domain to a subuser (read-only unless they authenticate their own domain). Returns the authenticated domain object with validation status and DNS record requirements. Side effects: Domain becomes available for sending email and requires DNS validation before full activation.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description is vague about what inputs are required and what the operation returns. Missing details about request body requirements and response structure. Lacks clarity on side effects (domain availability, DNS validation requirement). Enhanced description specifies required inputs, both subuser authentication options, return value, and important side effects for AI agent decision-making.

To associate an authenticated domain with a subuser, the parent account must first authenticate and validate the domain. The parent may then associate the authenticated domain via the subuser management tools.


[You can associate more than one domain with a subuser using the `v3/whitelabel/domains/{domain_id}/subuser:add` endpoint](https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/api-reference/domain-authentication/associate-an-authenticated-domain-with-a-subuser-multiple).
data.description_suggestion: Associates an authenticated domain with a subuser, allowing the subuser to send email using the parent account's authenticated domain. Requires domain_id path parameter and subuser username in the request body. The domain must be authenticated and validated by the parent account before association. Returns the association confirmation with domain and subuser details. Side effects: The subuser gains sending capability through this domain; the domain becomes the subuser's default sending domain unless they authenticate their own domain. Note: Use the bulk association endpoint to associate multiple domains with a single subuser.
data.reasoning: Summary is somewhat vague ('given user' is imprecise). Description lacks clarity on required inputs (subuser username), what the operation returns, and important side effects (default domain behavior, subuser's ability to override). Enhanced summary specifies 'subuser' for clarity. Enhanced description clarifies required parameters, prerequisites, return value, side effects regarding default domain assignment, and references the bulk operation for AI agent completeness.
